summary:
• Escalating Iran-US tensions and strikes in the Middle East are creating acute geopolitical supply risks, with potential for further disruption to Hormuz Strait transit and regional production if diplomatic talks continue to stall.

• Australian LNG production disruptions from labor strikes are beginning to impact global cargo loadings, adding to tighter supply conditions at a time when Asian energy demand remains robust and vulnerable to supply shocks.

• Weakening crude inventories and demand destruction pressures are masking underlying supply fragility, leaving markets exposed to price spikes if geopolitical flare-ups intensify or if additional production outages occur in key regions like the Gulf or South America.
